name: Leroy Cooper
abstract: Leroy "Hog" Cooper (August 31, 1928 – January 15, 2009) was an American jazz and R&B baritone saxophonist, most known for his 20-year association, some of the time as musical director/bandleader, with Ray Charles. From 1948 to 1951, Cooper toured with Ernie Fields' territory band.

works.t: The Band Wagon: Dancing in the Dark

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: I think Leroy was in the band about three years when he was inducted in the service, World War II, out of my band.

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Smith
about_quotes.evidence: They was all in my band. So we had six pieces. I had Cooper and Fathead.

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I was able to play with his band... I played with them in there
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
